answer: The `order-service` container will start only when the `rabbitmq` service is healthy. If the `rabbitmq` service is not healthy, the `order-service` container will not start.
Four distinct health checks are defined. Their common parameters are: test, interval (30s), timeout (10s), and retries (5).
The default infrastructure provider is Terraform, and the alternative provider is Bicep. This configuration is specified in the azure.yaml file.
The `store-front` container would fail to start if the `product-service` and `order-service` containers are not started first, as it depends on both of them.

Clone the sample application source from GitHub using git.  
Create a directory (e.g., demorepo), switch to it, and clone the repository with `git clone https://github.com/Azure-Samples/aks-store-demo.git`.  
Change into the cloned directory with `cd aks-store-demo`.  
If using azd, create an empty directory named aks-store-demo, change into it, and initialize the azd template with `azd init --template aks-store-demo`.  
Review the docker-compose-quickstart YAML file from the cloned repository.  
Use Docker Compose to build container images and start the application with `docker compose -f docker-compose-quickstart.yml up -d`.  
View created images with `docker images` and running containers with `docker ps`.  
Validate the running application by navigating to http://localhost:8080 in a web browser.  
Tools used: git, azd (if using azd template), Docker Compose, web browser.
